vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1316

plan.title: bug(run,scripts/_doctor_state): _render_doctor_status_line over-counts warnings by 1 on dirty throttle-skip when AGENTS.md freshness check emits severity=skip

narratives.Description: On a dirty throttle-skip, the doctor status line counts the AGENTS.md-freshness severity=skip finding as a warning, so the rendered tally is off by one (for example it shows 2 warnings when there is 1 warning plus 1 skip). This story excludes skip-severity findings from the persisted last_finding_count so the displayed warning count is correct, while decision logic, exit codes, and state persistence are otherwise unchanged.
narratives.Overview:
## Problem

`run` `cmd_doctor._render_doctor_status_line` (lines ~109-116) over-counts warnings by 1 on a dirty throttle-skip in repos where the AGENTS.md freshness check emits a `severity="skip"` finding (typical consumer repo with no `<!-- deft:managed-section v3 -->` markers; also the maintainer repo itself).

Flagged as a P2 (non-blocking style suggestion) by Greptile on PR #1315 review of `agent2/feat/1308-doctor-consolidation @ 8e0105a` -- merged into master via the swarm cascade after #1308 landed. The reviewer described it as: "The fix is trivial (filter skip-severity out of `finding_count` OR persist `warning_count` separately) but only affects the displayed warning tally on a dirty-skip line -- decision logic, exit codes, and state persistence are unaffected."

## Root cause

`_persist_doctor_state` writes `last_finding_count = len(findings)` after every full doctor run. The findings list now includes the `severity="skip"` entry the `agents-md-managed-section-fresh` check emits when a repo lacks the v3 managed-section markers. `_render_doctor_status_line` then computes:

```python
warns = max(decision.last_finding_count - decision.last_error_count, 0)
```

Which counts the skip entry as a warning, off-by-one on the user-visible status line.

## Impact

Display-only. Specifically:

- Decision logic (throttle 24h/4h, --full bypass, ritual-halt-on-persistent-dirty): unaffected.
- Exit codes: unaffected.
- State persistence: unaffected.
- `--json` `last_finding_count` and `last_error_count` fields: technically also include the skip entry, but JSON consumers can filter or just read severity.

User-visible symptom: dirty throttle-skip line on a consumer repo reads `1 error / 2 warnings -- UNRESOLVED` when the actual rendered findings list has 1 error + 1 warning + 1 skip. The off-by-one shows up only on dirty-skip; clean-skip is unaffected (silent of warnings).

## Resolution options

Pick whichever lands cleaner:

- **(a)** Filter skip-severity out of `last_finding_count` at persistence time in `_persist_doctor_state`. Cleanest because the count semantically means "findings that mattered", and skips never matter for the next-throttle decision.
- **(b)** Persist a separate `last_warning_count` field on `vbrief/.eval/doctor-state.json` and read it directly in `_render_doctor_status_line` instead of computing `last_finding_count - last_error_count`. More explicit; slightly bigger surface.
- **(c)** Filter skip-severity inside `_render_doctor_status_line` only -- leaves the persisted count semantically off but quickly fixes the display. Smallest diff, least clean.

Recommendation: (a). The state file is consumer-facing tooling, not just doctor-internal; "findings that mattered" is the right semantic.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `_render_doctor_status_line` displays the correct warning count on dirty throttle-skip for repos where the AGENTS.md freshness check emits a `severity="skip"` finding (maintainer repo + consumer repos with no `<!-- deft:managed-section v3 -->` markers).
- [ ] State persistence semantics documented (or adjusted) in `scripts/_doctor_state.py` docstring + `run` `cmd_doctor` docstring so future contributors know whether `last_finding_count` includes skip entries.
- [ ] Unit test added covering the dirty-skip + skip-severity-finding case end-to-end (probably in `tests/cli/test_doctor_throttle.py`).
- [ ] `task check` passes.
- [ ] CHANGELOG entry under `[Unreleased]` in #1242-compliant style (1-2 sentences -- this is a display nit, lead with the observable behaviour).

## Cross-references

- PR #1315 (the parent: `agent2/feat/1308-doctor-consolidation -> master`).
- Greptile review comment on PR #1315 that surfaced this P2 (id 4521525308; see the PR comments).
- #1308 (parent issue closed by PR #1315).
- `scripts/_doctor_state.py`, `run` `cmd_doctor._render_doctor_status_line`, `tests/cli/test_doctor_throttle.py` (the implementation surface).

## Out of scope

- Re-architecting the findings-list shape -- the skip severity is a real third state alongside warning/error, just needs to be counted (or not counted) consistently.
- Backporting to any pre-#1308 doctor implementation -- the consolidation IS #1308; this is a follow-up nit on the new code path.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
- In scripts/doctor.py::_persist_doctor_state, exclude severity=='skip' findings from the persisted last_finding_count so the count means 'findings that mattered' (resolution option (a) from the issue); _render_doctor_status_line then computes the warning tally correctly on a dirty throttle-skip line.
- Document the count semantics in the scripts/_doctor_state.py module docstring and the scripts/doctor.py cmd_doctor docstring so contributors know last_finding_count excludes skips.
- Add a unit test in tests/cli/test_doctor_throttle.py reproducing the dirty throttle-skip + severity=='skip' AGENTS.md-freshness finding case end-to-end; assert the rendered status line reports the correct warning count and the test fails before the fix.
- Add a CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] / ### Fixed entry in #1242 style referencing #1316.
narratives.UserStory: As an operator reading the doctor status line on a dirty throttle-skip, I want the warning tally to exclude skip-severity findings, so that the displayed count matches the real findings and I am not misled by an off-by-one.
narratives.Approach: 2026-06-17 file-overlap audit: touches scripts/doctor.py + scripts/_doctor_state.py + tests/cli/test_doctor_throttle.py only; disjoint from the other cohort carriers (reconcile script, triage-filter CLI, review-cycle skill, swarm skill). Display-only nit; decision logic / exit codes / state persistence semantics are otherwise unchanged. Parallel-safe; no depends_on.

items.title: Exclude severity=='skip' findings from persisted last_finding_count in scripts/doctor.py::_persist_doctor_state.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a full doctor run that emits 1 error + 1 warning + 1 skip finding, when _persist_doctor_state writes state, then last_finding_count excludes the skip entry (verified by tests/cli/test_doctor_throttle.py).

items.title: Status line shows the correct warning count on a dirty throttle-skip.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a dirty throttle-skip with a severity=skip AGENTS.md-freshness finding, when _render_doctor_status_line renders, then the warning tally counts only real warnings (asserted in tests/cli/test_doctor_throttle.py).

items.title: Document count semantics in scripts/_doctor_state.py and scripts/doctor.py docstrings.

narrative.Acceptance: Given the diff, when reviewed, then both docstrings state that last_finding_count excludes skip-severity findings.

items.title: Regression test covering the dirty-skip + skip-severity case.

narrative.Acceptance: Given the test suite, when pytest tests/cli/test_doctor_throttle.py runs, then it asserts the correct count and fails before the fix is applied.
Given the diff, when reviewed, then CHANGELOG.md has a 1-2 sentence [Unreleased] / ### Fixed entry referencing #1316.

references.title: Issue #1316: bug(run,scripts/_doctor_state): _render_doctor_status_line over-counts warnings by 1 on dirty throttle-skip when AGENTS.md freshness check emits severity=skip

expected_outputs: doctor status line warning tally correct on dirty throttle-skip
